preamble: Vocalization or lenition of voiced aspirated stops [bʱ] into glides [w] is a phonetic change often seen in rapid speech and as a diachronic shift in some Indo-Aryan dialects.

examples.note: In rapid speech of modern Indo-Aryan languages, voiced aspirated stops can weaken and resolve into glides.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
